entry.title: The Unity Architecture I Reuse Across Casual Game Projects
entry.content:
🧩 Why bother with a standard architecture?

After shipping several casual/hyper-casual projects, I learned that without a clear framework from day one, code turns messy fast — especially when handing off, extending features, or coming back to fix things months later. This post walks through the code structure I reuse in most of my projects, along with real code samples.

📌 Overall system diagram

🏗️ Core principle: the Controller is the single point of communication

The most important rule in this architecture: objects inside a scene are never allowed to call GUI/View directly. Every interaction between gameplay and UI must go through that scene’s Controller.

For example, inside GameController, every frame it pulls data from CarController and pushes it to GameView to render — CarController never calls GameView directly:

The benefit: gameplay logic stays fully decoupled from UI. Swapping the UI or adding a new view never requires touching gameplay logic.

🔁 The Singleton system — 3 flavors for 3 different lifetimes

I keep a single BaseSingleton.cs file defining 3 kinds of singleton, picked depending on the lifetime I need:

TypeBehaviorUsed for
Singleton<T>Plain class (where T : new()), creates its own instance lazilyPure logic classes that don’t need MonoBehaviour
ManualSingletonMono<T>Only returns an instance that already exists in the scene — never creates one, logs an error if missingManager classes that must be set up in a scene/prefab (UIManager, ResourcesManager, GameController…)
AutoSingletonMono<T>Automatically spawns a new GameObject if no instance exists yetUtility helpers that don’t need manual setup

The neat part: ManualSingletonMono<T> never calls DontDestroyOnLoad itself — whether it persists across scenes is entirely decided by where it’s instantiated (see the Entry Scene section below), not by the Singleton base class.

🚪 Entry Scene — where every persistent system gets bootstrapped

The very first scene (Entry) has one job: show a loading bar, then DontDestroyOnLoad a single manager prefab holding every system-level Manager, before switching to the Main scene.

Thanks to this, the managers below live for the entire lifetime of the app, independent of scene reloads:

  • UIManager — manages every Canvas in the game (View + Popup + Alert), spawned from the Entry scene, DontDestroyOnLoad.
  • ResourcesManager — stores and manages shared ScriptableObjects, DontDestroyOnLoad.
  • SoArchitectureManager — the event hub (Scriptable Object Architecture), DontDestroyOnLoad.
  • SaveDataHandler — reads/writes local save data (PlayerPrefs), DontDestroyOnLoad.
  • AudioManager — manages background music and sound effects, DontDestroyOnLoad.
  • GameConstants — shared constants: scene names, popup resource paths…

🎮 Controller — the brain of each scene

Unlike the Managers above, a Controller is a singleton but is NOT DontDestroyOnLoad — it only lives within its own scene (MainController for the Main scene, GameController for the Game scene).

The Controller’s job:

  • Acts as the only bridge between the current scene and the DontDestroyOnLoad Managers above (UIManager, SoArchitectureManager…).
  • Drives input, gameplay state (pause/resume/end game), and pushes data to the matching View.
  • Registers/unregisters events right inside RegisterEvents() / UnregisterEvents() — preventing listener leaks when switching scenes.

🖼️ The UI system: UIManager → ViewManager & PopupManager

UIManager

UIManager is a DontDestroyOnLoad singleton holding references to 3 sub-managers: ViewManager, PopupManager, AlertManager. It also handles the loading screen and scene-transition effects (using DOTween scale in/out).

View — a full-screen canvas per scene

UIViewManager manages Views (full-screen canvases per scene, e.g. MainView, GameView). Built-in features include:

  • A history stack of shown views (LastShownView) to support a Back button.
  • HideOthersView() auto-hides other views when showing a new one, avoiding overlapping UI.
  • OnShownCallBack / OnHideCallBack callbacks to keep the topbar and back button in sync.

Popup — toggle on/off and reused

UIPopupManager manages Popups (SettingPopup, ShopPopup, TutorialPopup, UpgradePopup…) using 2 dictionaries:

  • _dictPopup: caches created popup instances → reused instead of being recreated over and over.
  • _dictPopupControllers: dedicated controllers driving the show/hide/animation logic for each popup.

Each Popup inherits from UIPopup and overrides OnShowing() / OnHiding() to handle its own logic (e.g. SettingPopup pauses/resumes the game automatically when opened/closed).

📡 Data & Events: SoArchitectureManager, ResourcesManager, SaveDataHandler

SoArchitectureManager — an Observer pattern built on ScriptableObjects

Instead of systems calling each other directly (which quickly creates tangled dependencies), everything communicates through a GameEvent ScriptableObject:

Anything can AddListener/RemoveListener or Raise() without knowing who’s listening — reducing coupling between systems (Controllers, Views, and Popups can all listen to OnMoneyChangedEvent without holding a direct reference to each other).

ResourcesManager

A single place to store and load ScriptableObjects shared across the whole game (item data, config…), instead of scattering Resources.Load calls everywhere.

SaveDataHandler

Persists local data via PlayerPrefs + JsonUtility, auto-saving on app pause/quit/disable, and exposing RequestSave() to batch multiple changes and save once in Update() instead of saving on every single change (which would cause hitches):

🔊 AudioManager

Manages background music and sound effects (SFX), keeps separate volume settings for music/SFX, and is called directly by Popups/Views whenever needed (e.g. SettingPopup syncs the volume sliders, GameView plays an SFX on button press).

🧰 Shared Utilities

  • SimplePool — a lightweight object pool, avoiding repeated Instantiate/Destroy calls (extremely handy for bullets, coins, or any object that spawns constantly in a casual game).
  • SafeArea — automatically fits UI to the screen’s safe area on notched devices, attached directly to the RectTransform of whichever panel needs it.

✅ Summary: a full play session flow

  1. Entry scene → EntryController shows the loading bar, DontDestroyOnLoads the manager prefab, then loads the Main scene.
  2. Main scene → MainController shows MainView, waiting for the player to hit Play.
  3. Pressing Play → UIManager.Instance.ShowTransition() → loads the Game scene.
  4. Game scene → GameController shows GameView, registers events from SoArchitectureManager, and every frame pushes gameplay data (CarController) to GameView to render.
  5. Game over → GameController.ShowEndGame() → UIManager.Instance.PopupManager.ShowPopup(...).

Throughout this whole flow, every piece only talks to the others through Controllers + Managers + Events — no gameplay object ever reaches into the UI, and no UI ever reaches into gameplay.

🎯 When is this architecture worth it?

Best suited for:

  • Casual/hyper-casual projects that need to move fast with several simple scenes (Entry → Main → Game).
  • Small teams that need a shared standard new members can onboard onto quickly.

It can feel like overkill for a tiny 1-2 screen prototype — in that case, dropping the Controller/Event layer and calling things directly is often faster.

This post is based on the code framework I keep reusing across projects at Denk Studio.

entry.title: Pirate Run: Island Trader
entry.content:
🏴‍☠️ Pirate Run: Island Trader

Sail. Loot. Deliver. Survive.

Pirate Run: Island Trader is a 3D casual action game where you take the helm of your own pirate ship and plunder the open seas. Collect as many treasure chests as you can carry, dodge enemy waves and deadly hazards, then race back to deliver your haul before your ship goes under. One wrong move and you lose it all.

🎮 How to Play

  • 🕹️ Steer Your Ship: Use the on-screen joystick to navigate across the open ocean map.
  • 📦 Collect Chests: Sail over scattered treasure chests to pick them up. Your ship has a carry limit — plan your route wisely.
  • 🏠 Deliver Your Loot: Bring collected chests back to the delivery house to cash in and earn gold & gems.
  • ⚔️ Survive the Dangers: Enemy ships spawn in escalating waves, mines drift across the sea, and tornadoes roam and scatter your entire cargo.
  • 📈 Upgrade and Keep Going: Earn gold to upgrade your boat’s speed, capacity, and gold bonus. The longer you survive, the harder it gets.

✨ Key Features

  • 🚢 Multiple Boats to Unlock — Upgrade from a humble sloop to a powerful vessel with higher speed, more cargo capacity, and better durability.
  • 🗺️ Dynamic Treasure Spawning — Chests constantly respawn across the map in a smart grid system, keeping the seas full of opportunity.
  • 💣 Mines & Enemy Waves — Up to 15 mines drift across the ocean while enemy ships spawn in escalating waves, each announced before it hits.
  • 🌪️ Tornadoes — Roaming tornadoes scatter your entire cargo instantly — the most devastating hazard on the sea.
  • 🏝️ Island-Aware Map — Treasure never spawns on land. The world feels alive and fair.
  • 🏆 Endless High Score Mode — No timer. Your score is the number of chests successfully delivered. Beat your personal best every run.
  • 💰 Gold Bonus System — Invest in permanent upgrades between runs to multiply your earnings and push further each time.

📝 Overview

Pirate Run blends the tension of a resource-management game with the reflexes of an arcade runner. Every voyage is a risk-reward calculation: push deeper for more chests, or head back before the next wave hits? The escalating difficulty ensures no two runs feel the same.

Key Highlights:

  • 🎯 Risk-vs-reward decision making at its core
  • 🌊 Dynamic ocean with living hazards — mines, enemies, tornadoes
  • 🚢 Permanent progression through ship upgrades
  • ♾️ Endless replayability with personal best chasing

🖼 Screenshots

🛠 Technical Stack

ComponentTechnology / Tool
Game EngineUnity (3D)
Scripting LanguageC#
Target PlatformsAndroid, iOS
Graphics3D Stylized Pirate / Ocean Art
Gameplay MechanicsJoystick sailing, Cargo system, Wave AI
Level DesignProcedural chest spawn grid
Offline SupportFully Offline
UI AnimationDOTween
Version ControlGit + GitHub

Post by Benk Studio Team
Thientm27

entry.title: AnnoyPuzzle
entry.content:
🧠 AnnoyPuzzle — Looks Easy. Isn’t.

Drag. Drop. Regret your decisions. Repeat.

Welcome to AnnoyPuzzle, a deceptively simple puzzle game where every level is designed to make you think:

“There’s no way it’s this hard… right?”

Yeah… it is.

📝 Overview

AnnoyPuzzle is a drag-and-drop puzzle game that challenges your logic, creativity, and patience.

Each level presents a small problem.
Your job? Solve it using simple interactions.

Your reality?
Overthinking everything and still getting it wrong.

🎮 Gameplay

  • 👉 Drag objects into the correct position
  • 👉 Combine elements to unlock solutions
  • 👉 Experiment, fail, retry… a lot

💡 Not every solution is obvious.
Sometimes you’ll need to:

  • think differently
  • try the “wrong” move first
  • or question basic logic itself

🔥 Game Highlights

  • 🧠 Brain-teasing puzzles — simple idea, tricky execution
  • 🎯 Easy controls — just drag & drop
  • 😈 Light trolling mechanics — the game will mess with you
  • 📈 Progressive difficulty — it escalates quickly
  • 🎨 Clean & minimal design — no distractions, just pain

🖼 Screenshots

---

🛠 Technical Stack

ComponentTechnology / Tool
Game EngineUnity (2D)
LanguageC#
PlatformAndroid / iOS
Gameplay StyleDrag & Drop Puzzle
UI AnimationDOTween
BuildUnity Build Pipeline
Version ControlGit

🤡 Final Words

If you’re looking for a game that:

  • starts easy
  • gets confusing
  • and somehow makes you doubt your own intelligence

👉 AnnoyPuzzle is exactly that.

Good luck. Seriously.

entry.title: Denk Ultils
entry.content:
``

🧰 denk-unity-utils

A lightweight utility library for Unity 6, providing commonly used helpers for UI, math, transforms, collections,
enums, logging, and more.
All methods are static and ready to use out-of-the-box.

🔗 GitHub Repository:
https://github.com/DenkSuo/denk-unity-utils

📦 Table of Contents

SectionDescription
InstallationHow to install and use in Unity
DLoggerColored log system with tag & custom color
LogColors ReferenceList of available log colors
DateUtilsDate/time parsing and UTC conversion utilities
EnumExtensionsAdd string value attributes to enums
RectTransformExtensionsQuick utilities for UI anchoring and layout
UIMathMath and geometry helpers for UI and general calculations
UISetExtensionsSet UI values without triggering callbacks
MathExtensionsMath operations: clamp, repeat, abs, float↔byte conversions
TransformExtensionsTransform helpers for local/global positions
OtherExtensionsMiscellaneous handy helpers (Vector3, Color, etc.)
GameObjectExtensionGet all direct children
MaskExtensionsLayerMask contains check
ActionExtensionsNull-safe Action invoke
ArrayExtensionsShuffle, safe index, random element
BadWordExtensionBad word filtering
Credits & ContributionCredits and contribution info

Installation

  1. Download denk-unity-utils.unitypackage from the Releases page.
  2. Double-click the file to automatically import into Unity.
  3. Or install via UPM (local package):
    👉 Unity Docs

DLogger

Lightweight logging utility with colorized tags.
Ideal for debugging complex systems while keeping your console clean and readable.

Example Usage

Console Output (Unity-like)

Console Output (Unity-like)

Message Type
[DLogger] Hello World Info
[DLogger] Potential issue! Warning
[MySystem] Critical failure! Error

💡 Works only when #if !RELEASE is active — automatically disabled in release builds.
Combine with Scripting Define Symbols to control build-time logs.

LogColors Reference

Use the LogColors static class for consistent color tags in your logs.

Color Name Hex Code Preview
White`#FFFFFF`
Aqua`#00FFFF`
Aquamarine`#7FFFD4`
Turquoise`#40E0D0`
PaleGreen`#98FB98`
YellowGreen`#9ACD32`
Lime`#00FF00`
Chartreuse`#7FFF00`
GreenYellow`#ADFF2F`
LimeGreen`#32CD32`
Gold`#FFD700`
Orange`#FFA500`
OrangeRed`#FF4500`
Tomato`#FF6347`
Coral`#FF7F50`
DarkOrange`#FF8C00`
DeepPink`#FF1493`
Magenta`#FF00FF`
Violet`#EE82EE`
BlueViolet`#8A2BE2`
Orchid`#DA70D6`
MediumOrchid`#BA55D3`
---

DateUtils

Utilities for parsing, converting, and formatting date/time in UTC.

Highlights

  • Parse strings to DateTime
  • Convert milliseconds / seconds / days to UTC
  • Get current UTC timestamps
  • Format timespans and readable date strings

Methods

MethodDescription
ParseStringToDate(string str, string format)Parses a string into a DateTime object.
MilisecondToUtcDate(long milliSec)Converts milliseconds since epoch to UTC DateTime.
SecondToUtcDate(long second)Converts seconds since epoch to UTC DateTime.
DayToUtcDate(int day)Converts day count to UTC DateTime.
HourToUtcDate(int hours)Converts hour count to UTC DateTime.
UtcDayNow()Gets current UTC day number since epoch.
UtcSecondNow() / UtcMilisecondNow()Gets current UTC timestamp.
DateToUtcSecond(DateTime date)Converts a DateTime to epoch seconds.
ParseTimeSpanToHHMMSS(TimeSpan span)Formats TimeSpan as HH:mm:ss.
ToIso8601Weeknumber(DateTime date)Gets ISO week number.
DateToString(DateTime date)Returns compact string (shows only time if same day).
StartOfWeek()Returns UTC start of the current week.

EnumExtensions

Attach readable strings to enums and retrieve them easily.

Members

ItemDescription
EnumStringValueAttributeAttribute to store string labels for enum values.
GetStringValue<TEnum>(this TEnum enumValue)Extension method to fetch the string label or name.

RectTransformExtensions

A collection of helper methods to manipulate UI layout easily.

Example

Common Methods

MethodPurpose
SetDefaultScale()Sets localScale = Vector3.zero.
SetPivotAndAnchors(Vector2)Sets pivot and both anchors.
GetSize() / GetWidth() / GetHeight()Returns rect size info.
SetLeftTopPosition(Vector2) / SetRightBottomPosition(Vector2)Position by corners.
SetSize(Vector2) / SetWidth(float) / SetHeight(float)Resize.
Copy(RectTransform from)Copy layout & anchors.
FullScreen(bool)Match parent bounds.
Center(bool)Center in parent.
ResetAnchoredPosition3D() / ResetLocalPosition() / ResetLocalScaleToOne()Quick resets.
AnchorMinToZero() / AnchorMaxToOne() / CenterPivot()Anchor helpers.

UIMath

A compact collection of math helpers—great for UI, color conversion, and positioning.

Features

  • Safe Lerp, ClampIndex, RepeatIndex
  • Color ↔ Int/Hex conversion
  • Angle wrapping (WrapAngle)
  • Rect/UV ↔ Pixel conversions
  • DPI adjustments & pixel-perfect
  • Framerate-independent spring damp/lerp

Sample

UISetExtensions

Set values on UI elements without triggering OnValueChanged events.

Example

Supported Components

ComponentMethod
ToggleSet(bool value, bool sendCallback = false)
SliderSet(float value, bool sendCallback = false)
ScrollbarSet(float value, bool sendCallback = false)
DropdownSet(int value) (refresh UI)

MathExtensions

General-purpose math helpers for clamping, absolute values, range looping, and data conversion. :
contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

Features

  • Repeat() for looping integers or floats between bounds
  • Abs() / Clamp() / AbsClamp() chaining
  • ToInt() for rounded float-to-int
  • Convert float in range [-1,1] ↔ byte [0,255]

Methods

MethodDescription
Repeat(int value, int min, int max)Loops integer between min/max.
Repeat(float value, float min, float max)Loops float between min/max.
Abs(this float value) / Abs(this int value)Absolute value shortcut.
Clamp(this float value)Clamps between 0 and 1.
Clamp(this float value, float min, float max)Clamp with custom bounds.
AbsClamp()Applies Abs then Clamp.
ToInt()RoundToInt wrapper.
ToByte01() / ToFloat01()Encode/decode byte from normalized float.

Example

TransformExtensions

Helper methods for directly manipulating global, local, and anchored positions. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}

Features

  • Set global/local X/Y/Z directly
  • Work with RectTransform anchored positions
  • Traverse hierarchy safely
  • Fetch topmost parent with specific component

Common Methods

MethodDescription
SetGlobalX/Y/Z()Change world position axes individually.
SetLocalX/Y/Z()Change localPosition axes individually.
SetAnchoredX/Y/Z()Change anchoredPosition axes individually.
CheckParent(this Transform, Transform)Checks if transform is child of given parent.
GetTopmostParentComponent<T>()Finds highest ancestor with given component.

Example

OtherExtensions

Miscellaneous extra helpers for vectors, colors, and object manipulation (from OtherExtentions.cs).
(Summary below based on your script content.)

Highlights

  • Vector3/2 manipulation (normalize, clamp length, convert)
  • Color extensions for adjusting alpha or copying RGB
  • Random range and sign helpers
  • GameObject checks (IsDestroyed, SetLayerRecursively, etc.)

Example

GameObjectExtension (Children Helpers)

Helpers to quickly list direct children of a GameObject or Transform. :contentReference[oaicite:5]{index=5}

Methods

MethodDescription
GetAllChilds(this GameObject go)Returns List<GameObject> of all direct children.
GetAllChilds(this Transform trans)Returns List<Transform> of all direct children.

Example

MaskExtensions

Fast check if a layer is contained in a LayerMask. :contentReference[oaicite:6]{index=6}

Method

MethodDescription
LayerInMask(this LayerMask mask, int layer)true if layer belongs to mask.

Example

ActionExtensions

Null-safe helpers to invoke System.Action without repetitive null checks. :contentReference[oaicite:7]{index=7}

Methods

MethodDescription
SafeInvoke(this Action action)Invoke if not null.
SafeInvoke<P>(this Action<P> action, P p)Invoke with 1 param if not null.
SafeInvoke<P1,P2>(this Action<P1,P2> action, P1 p1, P2 p2)Invoke with 2 params if not null.

Example

ArrayExtensions

Collection helpers: shuffle, safe indexing, random choice. :contentReference[oaicite:8]{index=8}

Methods

MethodDescription
Shuffle<T>(this IEnumerable<T> list)Fisher–Yates shuffle (returns new IEnumerable).
GetSafe<T>(this T[] array, int index)Safe index access (default if out of range/null).
RandomChoice<T>(this List<T> list)Random element (Debug.LogError if null/empty).

Example

BadWordExtension

VN-friendly bad-word initialization, validation, and filtering utilities.
Supports diacritics and ignores interleaved symbols (e.g. b@a#d! still matches). :contentReference[oaicite:9]{index=9}

Quick Start

Methods

MethodDescription
Init(List<string> badWords)Prepares regex matchers once. (Must be called before use)
IsMatchAcceptChars(this string input)true if only allowed chars (VN diacritics, digits, spaces, _, etc.).
IsContainBadwords(this string input)true if any bad-word matched.
FilterBadwords(this string input)Replace matched words with *beep*.

Notes:

  • If not initialized or list empty → returns input unchanged.
  • Pattern tolerates symbols between letters to catch obfuscation.

Credits & Contribution

Developed and maintained by Benk Studio.
Tested and verified on Unity 6 (6000.0.28f1).

🧠 Contributions Welcome!
If you want to add more utilities, extensions, or optimizations — feel free to fork and submit a pull request!

© 2025 Benk Studio — MIT License
“Small, clean, and efficient utilities for modern Unity.”

entry.title: Wave Monster — Survive the Monster Waves!
entry.content:
👾 Wave Monster — Survive the Monster Onslaught!

Dodge. Shoot. Upgrade. Survive.

Welcome to Wave Monster — a thrilling casual survival game that challenges you to face endless waves of monsters. Test your reflexes and strategy as you dodge enemies, collect items, and power up to fight back!

📝 Overview

In Wave Monster, players step into a battlefield swarming with monsters. Your mission is simple: survive. Collect upgrades, dodge projectiles, and eliminate as many enemies as possible.

The game offers quick sessions of intense action, perfect for mobile gamers who love fast-paced challenges.

  • Non-stop wave-based action gameplay
  • Pickups and upgrades for weapons and abilities
  • Increasing difficulty with each wave
  • Unique enemies and colorful effects
  • Smooth controls and satisfying feedback

🔗 Demo / Download

👉 Play Demo on Web

🖼 Screenshots

🛠 Technical Stack

ComponentTechnology / Tool
Game EngineUnity (2D, WebGL build)
Scripting LanguageC#
BlockchainSolana (Wallet integration)
AuthenticationPhantom Wallet (Solana)
BackendMicrosoft Playfab
Data HandlingCloud Save, Leaderboards via Playfab
Target PlatformsWeb (Desktop, Mobile)
Graphics2D Fantasy Style
P2E SupportConfigurable Token Rewards
Version ControlGit + GitHub

Post by Benk Studio Team
Thientm27

entry.title: Idle Mechanics Manager — Build Your Repair Empire!
entry.content:
🔧 Idle Mechanics Manager — Rule the Garage!

Manage. Upgrade. Earn.
Become the ultimate repair shop tycoon in this fun and relaxing idle strategy game.

📝 Overview

In Idle Mechanics Manager, you’re the boss of a growing auto repair business. Hire skilled workers, fix vehicles, expand your garage, and rake in the profits — even when you’re offline. The game combines idle mechanics with satisfying management gameplay, making it perfect for casual players who love progress and strategy.

  • Repair and upgrade vehicles
  • Expand your shop operations
  • Unlock powerful upgrades and boosts
  • Earn while idle and automate your workflow
  • Vibrant 2D visuals in landscape mode
  • Web & Mobile (Telegram-ready)

🔗 Play Now

🚗 Available on Telegram Web Game
🪙 Supports Play to Earn + Airdrop system

🖼 Screenshots

🛠 Technical Stack

ComponentTechnology / Tool
Game EngineCustom Web Game Engine (Telegram)
Target PlatformsTelegram Web App, Mobile Browser
Art Style2D, Vibrant, Cartoon
Gameplay TypeIdle, Clicker, Tycoon
MonetizationPlay-to-Earn, Airdrop
Offline SupportYes
Backend / SyncTelegram / Cloud
Version ControlGit + GitHub

Post by Benk Studio Team
Thientm27

entry.title: Capybara Merge: Build Your Capybara Empire
entry.content:
🐹 Capybara Merge: Build Your Capybara Empire

Cute. Calm. Capybara Chaos.

Capybara Merge is a delightful hypercasual merge puzzle where you combine adorable capybaras to unlock new breeds and grow your ultimate capybara collection. Designed for mobile, the game blends relaxing visuals with light strategy, perfect for all ages and short sessions on the go.

🎮 How to Play

  • 🐾 Drag & Place: Drop capybaras into empty spaces on the board.
  • 🔁 Match & Merge: Combine two identical capybaras to create a new, higher-level one.
  • 🧠 Plan Ahead: The board fills quickly — think smart to keep it going.
  • 🎁 Unlock & Collect: Discover new capybara designs with every merge!

Charming animations and satisfying feedback make each merge feel rewarding, while new unlocks keep the progression exciting.

📝 Overview

From casual players to animal lovers, Capybara Merge offers a cozy gameplay loop with enough depth to keep you engaged. Whether you’re playing during a coffee break or commuting, the portrait mode ensures smooth one-handed gameplay.

Key Features:

  • 🧩 Hypercasual puzzle mechanics
  • 🦙 Adorable 2D capybara designs
  • 📲 Optimized for mobile (portrait)
  • 💤 Relaxing offline gameplay
  • 📈 Unlockable progression system with visual evolution

🔗 Demo / Download

👉 Coming Soon on Google Play

🖼 Screenshots

🛠 Technical Stack

ComponentTechnology / Tool
Game EngineUnity (2D)
Scripting LanguageC#
Merge SystemCapybara Evolution Tree Logic
PhysicsMinimal (Grid-based)
Target PlatformsAndroid
OrientationPortrait
Graphics2D, Cute Cartoon Style
Offline SupportFully Offline
Version ControlGit + GitHub

Post by Benk Studio Team
Thientm27

entry.title: Pocket Gladiators
entry.content:
⚔️ Pocket Gladiators

Fight. Upgrade. Earn.

Pocket Gladiators is a 2D PvE fighting game where players battle NPC opponents in intense 1vs1 matches. Each victory rewards gold, gems, and experience points — all used to gacha weapons, level up, and unlock character customizations.

This is a game that blends action with light RPG mechanics, enhanced with Web3 features for a Play to Earn experience.

🎮 Gameplay

  • 🥊 1v1 battles vs. AI: Fast-paced duels that test reflexes and timing.
  • 🏆 Victory rewards: Earn gold, gems, and XP after each win.
  • 🎲 Gacha system: Use in-game currencies to roll for powerful weapons.
  • 🎨 Customization: Change your character’s gear and appearance.
  • 🔁 Progression: Gain XP to level up and unlock higher difficulty fights.
  • 🎁 Daily login bonus: Come back daily for rewards and boosts.

The game’s level-based structure offers a casual but rewarding challenge curve, ideal for both quick sessions and long-term play.

🌐 Web3 Integration

  • 🪙 Built on Solana Ecosystem
  • 🧩 Login via Solana Wallet
  • 📦 Backend powered by Playfab (Microsoft)
  • 🪄 Token rewards & leaderboard potential
  • 📊 On-chain progression tracking (optional)

The blockchain layer supports future reward systems and NFTs while keeping core gameplay accessible.

📝 Overview

With tight 2D fighting mechanics, gacha progression, and full Web3 support, Pocket Gladiators stands at the intersection of arcade brawler and crypto-enabled reward systems.

Key Highlights:

  • 🎮 Skill-based combat with AI
  • 🎰 Gacha + leveling mechanics
  • 👕 Character gear & skin customization
  • 🌍 WebGL support for cross-platform access
  • 🪙 Play to Earn-ready with token integration
  • 🏅 Leaderboards and online stat tracking via Playfab

🎥 Demo Video

🖼 Screenshots

🛠 Technical Stack

ComponentTechnology / Tool
Game EngineUnity (2D, WebGL build)
Scripting LanguageC#
BlockchainSolana (Wallet integration)
AuthenticationPhantom Wallet (Solana)
BackendMicrosoft Playfab
Data HandlingCloud Save, Leaderboards via Playfab
Target PlatformsWeb (Desktop, Mobile)
Graphics2D Pixel/Cartoon Style
P2E SupportConfigurable Token Rewards
Version ControlGit + GitHub

Post by Benk Studio Team
Thientm27

entry.title: Dragon Dunk
entry.content:
🐲 Dragon Dunk

Addictive. On-chain. Earn as you play.

Dragon Dunk is one of the most addictive Play to Earn (P2E) dragon games built on the Solana blockchain. With simple but challenging gameplay, players guide a little dragon through circular obstacles to score points and earn rewards — all integrated seamlessly with Web3 systems.

🎮 Gameplay

  • 🔁 Endless dunk loop: Control the dragon to pass through rotating rings.
  • ⚡ Timing & precision: Points are scored only when you pass at the perfect angle.
  • 🎁 Earn while playing: In-game score links to earning mechanics on-chain.
  • 🔐 Login with Solana wallet: Secure and seamless Web3 authentication.

Designed to be easy to pick up but hard to master, the game is suitable for both casual gamers and crypto-savvy players.

🌐 Web3 Integration

  • 🪙 Built on Solana Ecosystem
  • 🧩 Login via Phantom Wallet
  • 📦 Backend powered by Playfab (Microsoft)
  • 🔗 On-chain player data and leaderboards (optional)

Blockchain elements are seamlessly merged with traditional game logic, ensuring both performance and decentralization.

📝 Overview

Whether you’re a fan of dragons, casual skill games, or on-chain experiences, Dragon Dunk brings it all together in a clean 2D package.

Key Highlights:

  • 🎮 Classic arcade gameplay with a Web3 twist
  • 🧠 Reaction-based challenge for all ages
  • 🌍 Cross-platform WebGL build
  • 📊 Online player tracking and earning history via Playfab
  • 🪄 Potential NFT/Token reward system (configurable)

🎥 Demo Video

🖼 Screenshots

🛠 Technical Stack

BlockchainSolana (Wallet integration)
AuthenticationPhantom Wallet (Solana)

Thientm27

entry.title: Fruit Merge: Make Giant Fruit
entry.content:
🍉 Fruit Merge: Make Giant Fruit

Drop, Merge, and Reach the Giant Watermelon!

Merge Fruit is a satisfying puzzle game inspired by the viral “Watermelon Game.” Your goal is simple: drop fruits into the bowl, match two of the same kind, and merge them into a bigger one. Keep going until you get the legendary watermelon!

🎮 How to Play

  • 🍎 Drag & Drop: Slide your finger to position the fruit.
  • 🍊 Match & Merge: Combine two same fruits to form the next-level fruit.
  • 🍉 Watch the Chaos: The bowl gets crowded fast — plan your merges carefully!
  • 💣 Game Over: When the fruits overflow the top of the bowl, it’s game over!

The physics-based movement and satisfying merge effect create an addictive experience that keeps players coming back.

📝 Overview

The game features a vibrant, cute 2D art style with juicy fruit designs. It’s lightweight, fully offline, and optimized for short casual play sessions.

Key Features:

  • 😄 Super easy to play
  • 🧠 Addictive and strategic as you go
  • 🍇 Supports leaderboard and local high scores
  • 🎨 Delightful art and bounce physics
  • 📴 Works offline – perfect for travel or downtime

🔗 Demo / Download

👉 Download on Google Play

🖼 Screenshots

🛠 Technical Stack

ComponentTechnology / Tool
Game EngineUnity (2D)
Scripting LanguageC#
Merge SystemCustom Merge Chain Logic
PhysicsUnity Physics2D
Target PlatformsAndroid
Graphics2D, Cute Fruit Cartoon Style
Offline SupportFully Offline
Version ControlGit + GitHub

Post by Benk Studio Team
Thientm27

entry.title: Doctor Dentist: Animal Teeth Care
entry.content:
🦷 Doctor Dentist: Animal Teeth Care

Be the dentist! Treat your animal patients and bring back their happy smiles.

Doctor Dentist is a colorful and engaging casual game for kids, where players become a dentist for cute animals like pandas, hippos, crocodiles, and more. It’s both fun and educational — teaching children about dental hygiene through playful interaction.

🧸 Gameplay Features

  • 🐼 Treat different animal patients with unique personalities and tooth problems.
  • 🦷 Use tools like toothbrushes, drills, pliers, and water jets.
  • 😁 Watch their expressions change as you clean, fix, and polish their teeth.
  • ✨ Bright and colorful 2D cartoon graphics that appeal to young audiences.
  • 🧠 Teaches kids about the importance of oral care in a fun, stress-free way.

📝 Overview

Doctor Dentist was designed with young players in mind. The UI is intuitive, the animations are expressive, and the sound effects are satisfying. It promotes gentle, relaxing play and encourages children to learn basic hygiene habits.

Highlights:

  • 🎮 Simple tap-based gameplay
  • 🪥 Encourages positive learning through play
  • 🎨 Kid-friendly art style and sounds
  • 📴 Works fully offline

🔗 Demo / Download

👉 Available soon on Google Play

🖼 Screenshots

🛠 Technical Stack

ComponentTechnology / Tool
Game EngineUnity (2D)
Scripting LanguageC#
UI & AnimationUnity UI + Spine
Level DataScriptableObjects
Asset OptimizationObject Pooling
Target PlatformsAndroid
Graphics2D, Hand-drawn Cartoon Style
Offline SupportYes

Thientm27

entry.title: Find Out: Spot Differences
entry.content:
🕵️‍♂️ Find Out: Spot Differences

Spot the difference. Train your brain. Relax and enjoy.

Find Out: Spot Differences is a casual brain-training puzzle game with three flexible gameplay modes, each offering a different way to challenge your observation and memory.

🧩 Game Modes

This game features 3 distinct modes, fully modular and easy to expand:

  • 🔍 Find the Difference: Compare two images and spot the subtle changes.
  • 🕵️ Hidden Object: Search and tap to find specific items hidden in cluttered scenes.
  • 🧠 Picture Puzzle: Reconstruct shuffled images by sliding or tapping pieces.

All modes are configurable using prefabs and ScriptableObjects, allowing easy level management and content updates without modifying the game logic.

📝 Overview

Each level features everyday scenes—such as stationery on desks, cozy bedrooms, food trays, and pets—designed in a charming 2D cartoon style. Puzzles are timed, but stress-free, ideal for players of all ages.

Benefits:

  • 🧠 Boosts concentration and memory
  • ⏱️ Tests visual scanning speed
  • 🎮 Supports short and casual play sessions
  • 📴 Fully offline – perfect for commuting or breaks

🔗 Demo / Download

👉 Download on Google Play

🖼 Screenshots

🛠 Technical Stack

ComponentTechnology / Tool
Game EngineUnity (2D)
Scripting LanguageC#
Gameplay ModesSpot Difference, Hidden Object, Puzzle
Level ManagementPrefabs + ScriptableObjects
Target PlatformsAndroid
Graphics2D, Hand-drawn Cartoon Style
Offline SupportFully Offline
AnalyticsFirebase (optional)
Ads MonetizationGoogle AdMob (optional)
Build SystemUnity Build Pipeline
Version ControlGit + GitLab

entry.title: Animal Rising: Fighting human
entry.content:
🐾 Animal Rising: Fighting human

A Play-to-Earn tower defense game where animals rise up to protect their forest from ruthless pirates. Built on the Solana ecosystem, Animal Defenders delivers real-time strategy gameplay combined with NFT elements and blockchain login integration.

🔥 Game Overview

The game tells the story of a battle between various animal species fighting against pirates who are attacking and destroying the forest. Players command a team of animals, deploying them strategically to defend their territory.

  • 🐵 Recruit unique animal heroes
  • 🪓 Fight against waves of pirate invaders
  • 🌳 Upgrade tree health and production
  • 💎 Earn rewards and progress through waves

🧠 Key Features

🔐 Solana Wallet Login

  • Blockchain login via Solana wallet
  • Data is securely managed through PlayFab (Microsoft backend)

📊 Upgrade System

Players can upgrade various attributes using in-game resources:

Animal Upgrades

  • Unlock and level up animals with special skills
  • Example: Backattack Gator – sneaks behind enemies and cripples them
  • Stats: ❤️ 35 HP | 🗡️ 5 DMG

Stage & Resource Upgrades

  • Increase food production rate and storage
  • Boost health for trees and the main home tree

🎮 Gameplay

  • Tap to deploy animals
  • Hold to advance forward
  • Manage food production and cooldowns
  • Each enemy wave becomes increasingly difficult

🔗 Demo / Download

🖼 Screenshots

🛠 Technical Stack

ComponentTechnology / Tool
Game EngineUnity (2D, WebGL build)
Scripting LanguageC#
BlockchainSolana (Wallet integration)
AuthenticationPhantom Wallet (Solana)
BackendMicrosoft Playfab
Data HandlingCloud Save, Leaderboards via Playfab
Target PlatformsWeb (Desktop, Mobile)
Graphics2D Fantasy Style
P2E SupportConfigurable Token Rewards

entry.title: Bird Dunk — A Fun Mobile Tap Game
entry.content:
🐦 Bird Dunk — Fun & Addictive Dunking!

1 player. Tap to fly. Endless fun.

Welcome to Bird Dunk — a charming, cartoon-style dunking game where players control a bird to aim and score through hoops. Perfect for quick casual play sessions!

📝 Overview

Take control of an adorable bird and finesse your shots through a series of hoops. With intuitive tap-and-release mechanics, score as many baskets as possible in a row to build combos and break your personal best.

  • Fast-paced and addictive gameplay
  • Cute and colorful 2D visuals
  • Easy to learn, hard to master!

🔗 Demo / Download

👉 Download on Google Play

entry.title: 2 Player Games: Dog Cat Battle
entry.content:
🐾 Paws & Claws — The Ultimate 2-Player Rivalry!

2 players. 1 device. Endless fun. One timeless rivalry…
Get ready to dive into the classic showdown between cats and dogs in Paws & Claws — a colorful, cartoon-style collection of casual PvP mini-games perfect for players of all ages!

📝 Overview

Battle it out in nearly 20 unique challenges that test your reflexes, strategy, and quick thinking — all on a single device, completely offline and free to play.
Whether you’re looking to spark some friendly rivalry between friends, siblings, or couples, these lighthearted games bring fast-paced action and laugh-out-loud moments.

🧩 Game Highlights

  • 🐶 Pate Bombers: Dinner is on the line! Drop bombs and outsmart your opponent to win the feast.
  • 🧙 Demon Slayers: Slash away evil spirits and claim your title as the ultimate exorcist.
  • 🏴‍☠️ Sea of Thieves: Set sail, collect treasure, and rule the seas in a pirate-themed face-off!
  • 🧟 Pet Pandemic: Zombies are coming — who can survive the longest in this end-of-the-world brawl?
  • 🐹 Ham Hoppers: Hop your way through the wasteland and fight for scraps!
  • 🌌 Star Collectors: Blast off and grab the shiniest stars in the galaxy.
    …and many more surprises waiting for you!

🔗 Demo (if available)

👉 Play Now

🖼 Screenshots

🛠 Technical Stack
